text: Our go-to before hitting the road. Green chile cheeseburger and a quick coffee, in and out before the long drive to drop Kai at her campus tours.
Reliable Gallup breakfast spot. Service is fast when you tell them you're heading out of town. Pancakes were solid.
Stopped here for an early bite before driving to Albuquerque. Good huevos rancheros, decent prices.
Exactly what I needed at 6am before a college-visit drive. Quick counter service, strong coffee, breakfast burrito to go.
Grabbed coffee here before the drive to UNM. Friendly barista, remembered my usual oat-milk latte on the second stop.
Good New Mexican plates close to the highway. Red chile enchiladas hit the spot, but the line at lunch is rough.
Cozy, clean, and fair prices. Easy quick stop on the way back from dropping Kai at a tour.
Best stop in Grants to break up the drive. Classic diner feel, no notes on the green chile stew.
Quick coffee stop halfway to Albuquerque. Exactly what I needed to stay sharp behind the wheel.
Right by UNM, perfect after a campus tour with Kai. Get the breakfast burrito and a cinnamon roll. The regulars know what's up.
Fast, cheap, and steady. We came back after the second UNM visit and it was just as good.
Solid Albuquerque diner. Reliable when we're in a hurry between campus stops. Decent green chile.
Good coffee downtown, slightly pricey but worth it before a long day of touring with my daughter.
Loved the atmosphere near Old Town. Sat down with Kai after her UNM tour and had a great lunch. Will come back.
Quick burger stop in Albuquerque. Green chile cheeseburger was excellent and they had us out fast.
Great little cafe walking distance from UNM. Kai loved it. Line at peak hours is rough but worth the wait.
Cozy and clean, prices are fair. A bit slow the morning we stopped before driving back to Gallup.
Classic Route 66 diner in Holbrook. Good midpoint stop on the long Flagstaff run. Patty melt did the job.
Quick stop on the way to Flagstaff for NAU. Exactly what I needed to break up the drive.
Decent coffee right off Hopi Drive. Fast counter, friendly staff. Good refuel before pushing on to Flagstaff.
Sat-down breakfast in Flagstaff before Kai's NAU tour. Came back twice this trip. Pie is the move.
Reliable when we're in a hurry. Quick breakfast and we made the campus tour with time to spare.
Best coffee in downtown Flagstaff. Great vibes, worth a stop before walking around the NAU area with my daughter.
Loved the atmosphere. Friendly baristas who remembered our order on the second day. Will bring Kai back.
Good dinner after a full day of touring NAU. Service was excellent and they remembered us from lunch.
Honest Flagstaff diner, fair prices. Quick stop before the long drive home to Gallup. No complaints.
Cozy and clean but a little slow. Prices were fair. Fine for a quick coffee on the way out of Flagstaff.
Best diner on Main Ave. Came back twice while Kai toured Fort Lewis. The hash browns are legendary, no notes.
Quick breakfast before the Fort Lewis campus visit. Exactly what I needed, friendly counter staff.
Great coffee on Main Ave in Durango. The regulars know what's up. Perfect stop before walking the Fort Lewis campus.
Solid local roaster, friendly staff, came back twice during our Durango trip. Good place to sit with Kai and plan the day.
Nice dinner in Durango after touring Fort Lewis. Reliable kitchen, good service even when busy.
Good burgers, slightly pricey for what you get but worth it once. Quick lunch between campus stops in Durango.
Favorite Gallup diner, hands down. We always stop here before or after a college-visit drive. Best green chile around.
Excellent service. Owner remembered our order on the second visit. Easy quick stop near home in Gallup.
Reliable when I'm in a hurry. Grabbed a quick breakfast before driving Kai to Albuquerque.
Decent Holbrook stop, fair prices. Line at peak hours is rough but the chicken-fried steak was solid.
Good roadside diner on Route 66. No frills but it gets you fed fast on the Flagstaff run.
Quick stop on the way home from UNM, exactly what I needed. Cheap, fast, and the green chile is the real deal.
Recommend the daily special. Sat down with Kai after a long campus day and it really hit the spot.
Solid local spot, friendly staff. A touch slow but fine for a quick bite between Albuquerque tours.
Consistent breakfast, reliable when I'm in a hurry before NAU tours. Pie still good, coffee a bit weak this time.
Good vibes, worth the stop every Flagstaff trip. Kai grabs a cold brew, I get a drip and we plan the campus walk.
Loved it last time but it was packed and slow on this Fort Lewis visit. Still a good Durango breakfast.
Excellent service, owner remembered Kai from our last drive through. Our standard pre-road-trip breakfast in Gallup.
Decent Grants stop, the line at peak hours is rough. Good place to stretch and eat halfway to Albuquerque.
Best black coffee in Gallup. I stop here most mornings before heading east. Staff know the order by now.
Coffee and a quiet corner - good for reading case notes.
Cocoa on cold days after a long practice.
Old west atmosphere is real. Steak and eggs breakfast is honest.
Chile is authentic and served hot. Owner remembers regulars.
Best red chile in town. Bring stretchy pants.
Green chile burger and a Coke after a long Friday. Bartender always cheerful.
Kai's birthday dinner spot. Reliable Chinese, good portions, family owned.
Birthday tradition since I was 8. Sesame chicken is the move.
Turkey club when I need something quick. Patio in warmer months.
Angela's place. Best breakfast burrito in town.
Green chile huevos are perfect after a night shift.
Homemade tamales, hard to find better in Gallup. Family recipe you can taste.
Solid Sunday lunch spot. Green chile enchiladas standard.
Best coffee refill service in Gallup. Angela's mom taught her right.
Country breakfast and diner atmosphere. Regulars know each other.
Green chile stew is exactly right. Not for the faint of heart.
Green chile double after cross-country practice.
Small place with big coffee. Owner grew up in Gallup.
Rocking chairs on the porch. Kai and I stop when we pass through.
Reliable pie, not much else. Good after a long shift.
Historic trading post. I bring visitors here. Angela and I know the family well.
Best selection of Navajo silversmiths represented anywhere. Legitimate provenance.
Quality trading post with fair prices. Third generation family business.
Best turquoise selection in Gallup. Perry knows every stone in the case.
Fills prescriptions fast. Pharmacist remembers regulars.
Copper's food and treats always in stock. Small but clean store.
Dr. Kaufman has cared for Copper since he was a pup. Compassionate and thorough.
Standard Home Depot. Good for shop supplies and F-150 parts.
Service department is slow but honest. Manager Randy helped me source F-150 parts.
Staff know their stuff. Randy the counter guy grew up wrenching.
F-150 parts they don't have they'll order in two days.
Convenient. Nothing special but they had the Painless wiring bracket I needed.
Cheaper on some Ford parts than O'Reilly. Regular customer discount.
Sunday morning grocery run stop. Familiar staff.
Kept me stocked for years. Green chile roasting event in autumn is the highlight.
Better produce section than Safeway. I alternate weeks.
Necessary for bulk supplies. Late night hours help after long shifts.
Kai's running shoes and hiking gear. Worth the drive from Gallup.
Ammunition and camping gear. Better prices than Gallup on marksman supplies.
Silver stock and casting supplies. Worth the two-hour drive quarterly.
Aoki brought me here when I was starting. Family shop.
Angela runs one of the best galleries in Santa Fe. She has represented my work three years.
Angela has an eye for real work. Recommended without reservation.
Convenient stop before precinct meetings. Quiet corner tables.
Sam's spot. Now it's my spot too.
Angela's grilled cheese is my after-school move.
Pork tamales are the standard. Family owned for decades.
Best diner in town. Breakfast tacos every Tuesday.
Green chile stew is my personal favourite in Gallup.
Ellis is family and represents Sam's work honestly.
Perry cuts and shapes stones as well as any smith. My students go there.
Dr. Kaufman is kind to Copper. He always brings treats.
Slow but competent. Sam's F-150 parts source.
Good place to meet a source. Never rushed out.
Green chile stew after a long shift. Nothing better.
Good for wings on a Friday. Loud on game night.
Green chile cheeseburger after cross country.
Enchiladas Christmas style. Solid.
Best sterling supplier in the state. Fair pricing on findings.
Been coming here for stones since 1998. Family run and honest.
Angela runs a proper gallery. Show up ready to talk work.
Well stocked. Grabbed new boots before a Zuni hike.
Best dark skies in the Southwest. Bring the Celestron.
Small selection but they carry Alien Ale which is enough.
Pancakes after a Saturday practice. Quiet mornings.
